---
title: "Publications"
format: html
---
## Selected Work

**Brissa Acevedo**, Baruch Fischhoff, and Lia Patrício. *Community preferences in energy justice: Perspectives on solar farm projects.* Accepted for publication in **Energy Research & Social Science**.



## Policy Briefs
::: {.callout-note}

### Do Renewables Really Raise Electricity Prices? What the Data Shows
Policy breif examining the relationship between renewable energy adoption and electricity prices using 25 years of U.S. data (2000-2024). Analysis finds no consistent correlation between renewable generation and price increases, challenging common assumptions about renewable energy costs.

[Read the article](https://ace-usa.org/blog/research/research-environmental-policy/do-renewables-really-raise-electricity-prices-what-the-data-shows/){target="_blank"}
:::

::: {.callout-note}
### LIHEAP at a Crossroads: What's Next for Federal Energy Assistance to Low-Income Households?

Policy brief examining the debate over LIHEAP (Low Income Home Energy Assistance Program) and the federal government's role in helping low-income households pay energy bills. Explores arguments on both sides and what's at stake.

[Read the article](https://ace-usa.org/blog/research/research-environmental-policy/liheap-at-a-crossroads-whats-next-for-federal-energy-assistance-to-low-income-households/){target="_blank"}
:::
